content.problemStatement:
We have a rectangular piece of cardboard with width 111 (its height is not important). We are going to fold it in the following way:


pick up the right side of the piece of cardboard and move it to the left until it touches the left side
this way, a smaller piece of cardboard with width 1/21/21/2 is created; if we unfolded it, we could see a vertical ridge formed in the middle
pick up the left side of this new piece of cardboard and move it to the right until it touches the (new) right side
pick up the right side of the resulting piece of cardboard and move it to the left until it touches the left side, etc.
Whenever the cardboard is folded, exactly one of its new sides is a newly formed ridge (there may be more, internal ridges formed, but we do not consider these). Let's denote such a ridge created in the kkk-th folding by RkR_kRk​.

In total, we fold the piece of cardboard NNN times. Afterwards, we unfold it and look at the formed ridges. Let's denote the distance of ridge RNR_NRN​ (i.e. the last formed outer ridge) from the left side of the original piece of cardboard by DND_NDN​. For example, D1=1/2D_1 = 1/2D1​=1/2 and D2=1/4D_2 = 1/4D2​=1/4.

It is possible to express DND_NDN​ as an irreducible fraction x/yx/yx/y. Find this fraction.

Assume that it is possible to fold the piece of cardboard as many times as we want.
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. For each test case, a space and an integer NNN follows.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing 2T2T2T space-separated integers. For the iii-th test case (1≤i≤T1 \le i \le T1≤i≤T), the 2i−12i-12i−1-th and 2i2i2i-th integer should denote xxx and yyy — the position of the last ridge as an irreducible fraction.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We only fold once, so x=1x=1x=1 and y=2y=2y=2.
Example case 2: We fold the piece of cardboard twice. The last edge is at 1/41/41/4, so x=1x=1x=1 and y=4y=4y=4.
